macro so today's Q macro question is how
much more pain will global divergences
cause our answers are today starts a
second consecutive week in which
investors will be barraged by litany if
critical macro and policy data on
balance we expect the data to support
three Divergence themes that have
contributed to the recent poor
performance across asset markets number
one US exceptionalism on the growth
front number two sticky reported
inflation relative to doish forecasts
among Wall Street Economist and central
banks and number three marginally honish
fed and boj policy versus marginally
honish or marginally doish ECB and pboc
policy those divergences have
contributed to the hawkish repricing of
the treasur market and by extension
Global fixed income markets the 10-year
nominal treasury will reached the
highest level since the fall of 2023
this morning these divergences have
contributed to US dollar strength the US
dollar Index reached the highest level
since the fall of 2022 this morning what
is different today relative to the fall
of 2022 and the fall of 2023 is the
positioning cycle is currently in an
extreme bullish condition meaning
investors are betting on upside risk in
Risk assets to an asymmetric degree
investor positioning was in an extreme
bearish condition in the fall of 2022
and fall of 2023 what is also different
about today than those periods is risk
assets have not aggressively priced in
the reduction in liquidity that these
hawkish developments imply risk assets
appear likely to catch down to where the
multi-month downtrend in the 42 macro
Global liquidity proxy suggest it should
be transitioning to our 42 macro or
dashboard here is always a wrap up with
the question from our community this
one's regarding the 102 treasury yield
spread says hiar darus the spread was
inverted for about two years before
Rising slightly above the zero mine zero
line for a few months then started a
more decisive rise around mid December
2024 and it's now 38 basis points above
the zero line historically that behavior
has four to recessions but I believe the
economic indicators are poined a slower
growth not a recession that is correct
what is your interpretation of the 10
two spread unlike the run up to Prior
recessions the two-year yield is trading
sideways rather than Divi decisively
trending down it would appear the
two-year recognizes the resiliency of
the economy and that the FED is not
looking to aggressively cut rates that
is also correct is is it possible that
the 10-year yield is the main driver of
the yeld curve spread because it is
anticipating higher inflation ahead so
excellent question uh very well
contextualized uh um and and so a lot of
the assumptions in the question were
were correct uh in my opinion uh with
respect to the 10e Y being the main
driver of this so right now we are in a
bearish steepening of the Y curve that
just means that bonds are going down as
the Y curve go steepens or the the
security prices are going down as the
yield curve steepens um and this is a as
as the question implies a function of
the reduction in in policy rate easing
that was priced out of the Ford rate
curve in recent months so that is
certainly a big driver and the fact that
it is being the tenure is rising uh does
not necessarily mean it's a sign of
higher inflation ahead it could be a
sign of higher inflation ahead it could
be a sign of higher Rising nominal
growth expectations it could also be a
sign of rising term Premia of which turn
premum have accounted for about 80 plus
percent of the rise in 10year treasury
yields off the cycal low that we
observed back in um early to mid
September prior to the feds uh starting
to um to cut the policy rates so um this
Big Driver this big expansion of ter
Premia which by the way is still well
shy of its historical mean um he got
quite a quite a quite quite a ways to go
in that regard if we were going to get
back to those historical means anytime
soon uh this big expansion of ter premum
in our opinion is being driven by fiscal
policy concerns with respect to the
likely widening of us uh budget deficits
uh relative to the Baseline over the
long term now again do is going to uh uh
find uh uh uh Avenues to pockets of of
federal expenditures to cut uh um you
know in order to sort of balance uh uh
to offset some of the uh Revenue
reduction that we're going to see uh as
a function of extending and expanding of
the Trump tax cuts uh there's also going
to be tariffs as a as a as a revenue
Razer to offset some of that stuff as
well because don't forget if you're
going to use reconciliation to you know
change fiscal policy the the the changes
cannot have an impact on the deficit on
a 10-e four period that's that's part of
the bird rule up in the Senate so
they're going to have to find
expenditure reductions and revenue
erasers in my opinion in the form of
tariffs to kind of um to offset that but
the key takeaway is that relative to the
current Baseline of current law this
stuff is going to wind up with a deficit
expansion given that again current law
has the Trump tax cuts expiring at the
end of 2025 so just extending them is
going to add trillions of dollars of
incremental debt to the US public sector
balance sheet over the course of the
next 10 years so again they're going to
have to find Avenues to offset that from
a bird rule accounting perspective so
but again in our opinion this is why the
ter premum have won so much recently
because you know we were starting to see
the whites of the eyes on some of the um
some of these sort of fiscally
expansionary policies that we are likely
to get uh uh in the coming months if not
